evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` combines normalization/logging (`normalizeAppError`, `logAppError`) with browser event-bus notifier lifecycle (`configureErrorNotifier`, `window.addEventListener`, module-level singleton state), creating a high-traffic mixed-responsibility hub.

`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` centralizes UA parsing, pointer/orientation detection, multiple independent hooks, and a composite `useDeviceInfo` API in one file, making it a broad cross-feature ownership point rather than a focused boundary.

`src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/scenarios.ts` and `src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/visuals.tsx` share a string-key protocol (`InstallVisualKey`) coordinated by condition trees and a large registry, so adding/changing scenarios requires synchronized edits across multiple modules.

`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` includes both primitive wrapper exports and touch-specific open/close orchestration (`TouchableTooltip`), blending base design-system concerns with interaction policy.

Prop-bag resolvers flatten multiple interfaces with object spread in `resolveShotImagesEditorProps` and `resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps`, creating silent key-collision risk at integration boundaries (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:174`, `src/shared/components/JoinClipsSettingsForm/types.ts:106`).

Cross-module shot-selection contract diverges: event bus emits `selectShotForAddition` with `{ shotId, shotName }`, while context API only accepts `shotId: string`, forcing adapters/drop of payload fields (`src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts:18`, `src/shared/contexts/ShotAdditionSelectionContext.tsx:5`).

Orchestration metadata contract is weakly typed (`Record<string, unknown>`) while consumers parse multiple legacy paths, indicating boundary drift instead of a single canonical handoff (`src/shared/lib/tasks/orchestrationContract.ts:15`, `supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:36`).

Edge-function operation style is inconsistent: shared `EdgeOperationResult` helpers exist, but storage upload path still throws raw exceptions while other paths return structured failures, producing mixed seam behavior (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts:19`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts:75`).

Task↔generation mapping logic is duplicated across modules (`tasks[0]` extraction in two places), increasing risk of drift in cross-layer assumptions (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts:43`, `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:239`).

src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/utils/image-utils.ts defines getAspectRatioStyle() as a pure pass-through to resolveAspectRatioStyle() in src/shared/lib/imageAspectRatio.ts, adding an extra wrapper layer with no behavior.

supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts computes objectPath in handleStorageOperations(), then recomputes the same taskOutput path again inside handleThumbnail() instead of consuming the already-determined value.

src/domains/generation/types/generationMetadata.ts and src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/legacyMigrations/legacyProjectImageSettings.ts both allow open-ended keys ([key: string]: unknown / extends Record<string, unknown>), which keeps migration residue representable indefinitely.

src/shared/lib/preloading/preloader.ts swallows preload failures with an empty catch and does not record failed IDs/URLs, while supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts has multiple best-effort catches that also suppress failure context.

Task-creation modules repeat the same control-flow skeleton (validate -> resolve/build params -> createTask -> catch handleError+rethrow) in imageGeneration, magicEdit, and joinClips rather than reusing a shared pipeline abstraction.
orchestration_contract assembly is duplicated across task builders (imageGeneration, magicEdit, joinClips, travelBetweenImages payloadBuilder) with repeated inline field wiring.

useInlineEditState.ts introduces multiple pass-through wrapper hooks/functions (environment/persistence/inpainting/magic/reposition/img2img/settings sync/model builder), with large mapping layers that mostly repackage collaborators rather than simplify behavior.
useMediaGalleryController.ts is largely an argument reshaping layer around useMediaGalleryHandlers/useMobileInteractions, adding a hop without strong behavioral ownership.
DescriptionModal passes onClose directly to Dialog onOpenChange (boolean callback), but DescriptionModalProps defines onClose: () => void, so the runtime callback shape and declared interface differ.

buildOrchestrationContract returns Record<string, unknown>, while downstream payload builders treat the contract as a stable schema; this weakens compile-time guarantees at a critical cross-system boundary.
useAdjacentPagePreloader swallows prefetch failures in empty catch blocks for both next and previous page branches, unlike nearby modules that route failures through handleError.

imageGeneration createBatchImageGenerationTasks uses DEV-only console.error for per-task failures while production paths rely on handleError; error reporting behavior changes by environment and call site.

Server-side handlers (e.g., complete_task/generation.ts and calculate-task-cost) use direct console.error patterns while client modules standardize through handleError, leading to inconsistent context propagation conventions across boundaries.

findings.summary: Global error entrypoint mixes core error normalization with UI notification transport and runtime singleton wiring.
evidence: `handleError.ts` owns both error domain logic and browser event notifier state (`registeredNotifier`, `removeNotifierListener`, `window.dispatchEvent`).
Feature hooks call this shared hub directly (`useMobile.tsx`, `useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`), so UI-notification transport and core error semantics are coupled through one widely imported surface.
findings.suggestion: Split into `errorCore` (normalize/log/typed AppError) and `errorNotifications` (browser/event transport). Keep `handleError` as a thin facade or move notifier registration to app bootstrap to reduce shared-layer ownership overlap.

findings.summary: Device detection responsibilities are concentrated in a single shared hook module with broad, cross-cutting API surface.
evidence: `useMobile.tsx` contains UA regex heuristics, pointer/orientation helpers, three focused hooks, `isMobileUA`, and composite `useDeviceInfo` in one file.

UI modules consume `useIsMobile` (`CreativePartnerPane.tsx`) while tooltip logic separately implements touch interaction behavior (`tooltip.tsx`), indicating responsibility spread and unclear ownership boundary for device-policy decisions.
findings.suggestion: Decompose into focused modules (e.g., `deviceSignals`, `mobileHooks`, `orientationHooks`) and keep shared UI components consuming a single policy API instead of embedding additional device-specific behavior.

findings.summary: Install instruction flow relies on manually synchronized scenario logic and visual registry keys across separate files.
evidence: `scenarios.ts` returns string literals (`resolveInstallVisualKey`) that must match `InstallVisualKey` and `installVisualRegistry` in `visuals.tsx`.

`InstallInstructionsModal.tsx` resolves title and visual through separate lookups, so a new platform/browser path can drift between decision logic and visual mapping without a single source-of-truth object.
findings.suggestion: Replace split conditional+registry contracts with one typed scenario table (conditions + title + visual component) exported from a single module, then have the modal consume one resolved scenario object.

findings.summary: Shared tooltip module contains both base primitives and app-specific touch interaction policy, blurring design-system ownership.
evidence: `tooltip.tsx` exports primitive wrappers (`Tooltip`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent`) and also `TouchableTooltip` with pointer/touch event orchestration and outside-tap behavior.

Feature components still implement custom open-state control (`CreativePartnerPane.tsx`, `InfoTooltip.tsx`), indicating the shared layer is neither purely primitive nor a consistent policy abstraction.
findings.suggestion: Keep `shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` strictly primitive wrappers; move `TouchableTooltip` into a feature-agnostic behavior package or feature-local component so shared UI boundaries stay consistent.

findings.summary: Inline edit state orchestration is over-centralized, creating a hard-to-reason local control flow surface.
evidence: `useInlineEditState.ts` spans environment init, persistence, mode hooks, settings sync, side effects, and final shape adaptation in one large hook.
The return object exposes multiple deeply nested state groups (`canvasEnvironment`, `inpaintingState`, `generationState`, etc.), increasing mutation and wiring complexity.
`InlineEditView.tsx` depends on this broad surface, so UI and orchestration remain tightly coupled.
findings.suggestion: Split `useInlineEditState` into narrower composable hooks by responsibility (environment, edit modes, persistence sync, action handlers) and expose a smaller facade consumed by the view. Keep `useImageEditValue` as a thin adapter or remove it by converging on one state contract.

findings.summary: Task payload creation/validation seams are duplicated across task families, causing glue-code entropy.
evidence: Each module separately performs required-field checks, defaults, orchestration contract injection, seed handling, and payload shape assembly.
Cross-family behavior (validation style, default policy, param naming) is repeated with local variations instead of a shared boundary layer.
This makes changes to orchestration metadata or common task fields require broad ripple edits.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared task-request assembler layer (common validation/default/orchestration envelope + family-specific payload plugins) so each task family only supplies its unique fields.

findings.summary: Realtime/polling policy module directly depends on UI toast behavior, blurring subsystem boundaries.
evidence: `DataFreshnessManager.ts` imports and calls `toast.error` inside fetch-failure policy handling.
`useTasks.ts` delegates fetch success/failure into this manager, so data-layer orchestration implicitly triggers presentation-layer side effects.
This reduces reuse/testability of freshness policy in non-UI contexts and makes failure behavior less predictable at the seam.
findings.suggestion: Move toast emission behind an injected notifier or event callback interface; keep `DataFreshnessManager` pure to freshness/polling state and let UI subscribers decide notification behavior.

findings.summary: Button group prop construction uses repetitive wrappers and ineffective memoization.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps.ts` repeats the same shared fields in four nearly identical builders.
The hook memoizes with `[params]`, but callers typically pass new object literals, so recomputation still occurs.
This adds indirection without meaningfully reducing complexity in lightbox render assembly.
findings.suggestion: Replace the four builders with a single shared-base object plus per-group extension, and memoize on explicit primitive/sub-object dependencies rather than the whole `params` object.

findings.summary: Join clips test mocks an outdated error module path, weakening test honesty at the abstraction boundary.
evidence: Production module imports `handleError` from `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`.
The test mocks `@/shared/lib/errorHandler`, so the intended dependency is not intercepted.
This allows tests to pass without asserting real error-path behavior for the current seam.
findings.suggestion: Align the mock path with the actual import and add an assertion that error handling is invoked for at least one failing branch.

findings.summary: Composite prop resolvers flatten multiple bags via spread, making boundary contracts collision-prone and non-obvious.
evidence: `resolveShotImagesEditorProps` returns `{...displayOptions, ...imageState, ...editActions, ...shotWorkflow}` with no collision guard (`types.ts:174-180`).
`resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps` uses the same flattening pattern across three bags (`types.ts:106-111`).
Both APIs expose many optional/sharedly named fields (e.g., callbacks and mode/state values), so additions can silently override existing fields at integration seams.
findings.suggestion: Keep grouped prop namespaces through render boundaries (pass `displayOptions`, `imageState`, etc. as nested objects) or enforce compile-time collision detection with explicit mapped types and `satisfies` checks before merge.

findings.summary: Shot-addition selection handoff is inconsistent between typed events and context API, causing lossy adaptation at module boundaries.
evidence: Event contract defines `selectShotForAddition: { shotId: string; shotName: string }` (`typedEvents.ts:18`).
Context action only accepts `selectShotForAddition(shotId: string)` and stores only ID (`ShotAdditionSelectionContext.tsx:5`, `:19-21`).
The seam currently has two source-of-truth shapes for the same domain action.
findings.suggestion: Define one shared DTO for shot-selection payload and reuse it in both the event map and context API; either include `shotName` in context state or remove it from event payload to eliminate drift.

findings.summary: Orchestration handoff lacks a canonical typed contract, forcing downstream multi-path parsing and legacy branching.
evidence: `buildOrchestrationContract` returns `Record<string, unknown>` rather than a typed contract object (`orchestrationContract.ts:17-32`).

`extractOrchestratorRef` checks multiple paths (`orchestration_contract`, `orchestrator_task_id_ref`, nested `orchestrator_details`, `originalParams`, direct field) to recover one reference (`billing.ts:40-51`).
This indicates producers/consumers are not aligned on one boundary format.
findings.suggestion: Promote a shared `OrchestrationContract` interface used by all producers and edge consumers; write/parse only that structure and keep legacy extraction in a temporary migration shim with explicit sunset.

findings.summary: Edge modules mix structured result objects and thrown exceptions for operational failures, creating inconsistent error/lifecycle propagation across function seams.
evidence: Shared edge helpers define `EdgeOperationResult` and `edgeFailure/edgeSuccess` as a standard boundary contract (`edgeOperation.ts:19-47`).
`handleStorageOperations` throws raw `Error` on upload failure (`storage.ts:75-77`) while thumbnail sub-flow returns `EdgeOperationResult` (`storage.ts:108-153`).
Within one module, callers must handle two failure channels (throw vs result object).
findings.suggestion: Normalize storage operations to return `EdgeOperationResult` for all recoverable/non-recoverable outcomes and let top-level handlers translate once to HTTP response behavior.

findings.summary: Generation-to-task boundary rules are duplicated in separate modules, risking divergent behavior when task association semantics evolve.
evidence: `generationTaskBridge` resolves primary task as first entry of `tasks` array (`generationTaskBridge.ts:43-45`, `:66-68`).
`generationTransformers` independently implements `extractTaskId` with similar first-item logic plus string fallback (`generationTransformers.ts:239-245`).
No shared helper enforces one mapping rule for this cross-module seam.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single `getPrimaryTaskId(tasks)` utility (or shared mapper) used by both bridge and transformers, then cover it with focused tests for array/string/null/empty edge cases.

findings.summary: Timeline write serialization and generation invalidation scheduling are separate mechanisms with no explicit coordination contract, allowing stale refresh timing races.
evidence: `timelineWriteQueue` tracks per-shot active/queued writes via module-level maps and exposes `isTimelineWriteActive` (`timelineWriteQueue.ts:86-102`).
`useGenerationInvalidation` schedules delayed invalidations and immediate refetches without consulting timeline write activity (`useGenerationInvalidation.ts:72-93`, `:104-114`, `:140`).
Two subsystems mutate/read the same shot data lifecycle but do not share a seam-level guard contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared coordination API (e.g., invalidation defers while `isTimelineWriteActive(shotId)` or queue emits completion event) and route both modules through that API to avoid stale-overwrite races.

findings.summary: Aspect-ratio logic is split by a no-op wrapper, adding indirection without behavior.
evidence: getAspectRatioStyle() in image-utils.ts only returns resolveAspectRatioStyle(projectAspectRatio) with no transformation.
Core implementation already exists in imageAspectRatio.ts, so call sites carry two names for one behavior.
findings.suggestion: Remove getAspectRatioStyle() and call resolveAspectRatioStyle() directly, or move any intended ShotImageManager-specific behavior into the wrapper so the extra layer is justified.

findings.summary: Thumbnail generation recomputes storage object paths instead of reusing the already-selected upload path.
evidence: handleStorageOperations() sets objectPath using storagePaths.taskOutput(...).
handleThumbnail() rebuilds objectPath again from userId/taskId/filename before calling generateThumbnail(), duplicating path derivation logic.
findings.suggestion: Pass objectPath from handleStorageOperations() into handleThumbnail() and stop rebuilding it there; keep a single source of path truth per request.

findings.summary: Open-ended metadata typing preserves legacy fields indefinitely and weakens migration cleanup boundaries.
evidence: GenerationMetadata includes [key: string]: unknown, allowing any ad-hoc metadata key beyond documented fields.
LegacyProjectImageSettings extends Record<string, unknown> while also carrying @deprecated fields and a sunset date, so unknown legacy keys remain structurally valid after migration windows.
findings.suggestion: Replace broad index signatures with explicit extension points (e.g., a nested extra metadata bag) and add strict migration-boundary parsing to reject unknown legacy keys after the removal gate.

findings.summary: Best-effort flows suppress failures without recording actionable state, causing opaque behavior and potential repeated work.
evidence: preloader.ts catches queue.add() errors and ignores them completely, so callers cannot distinguish failed vs skipped image preloads.
storage.ts catches errors in generateThumbnail() and cleanupFile() with fallback/console behavior only, dropping structured failure details from function outputs.
findings.suggestion: Track and return lightweight failure metadata in best-effort paths (failed IDs/counts/reasons) so retries and observability are intentional rather than implicit.

findings.summary: Domain workflows are placed in `shared/hooks`, blurring ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useAutoTopup` handles billing persistence/auth/session and edge-function orchestration (`setup-auto-topup`) rather than cross-domain reusable behavior.
`useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator` owns generation thumbnail queue policy and generation-query cache updates, which are specific to the generation subsystem.
findings.suggestion: Move these hooks into domain-owned folders (for example `src/domains/billing/hooks/useAutoTopup.ts` and `src/domains/generation/hooks/useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts`) and keep `shared/hooks` for domain-agnostic primitives only.

findings.summary: Generation-specific UI components are exposed from `shared/components` as if globally reusable.
evidence: `ActiveLoRAsDisplay` depends on LoRA domain types and metadata conventions (`ActiveLora`, `LoraDisplayModel`, `Model ID`, trigger-word insertion behavior).
`AspectRatioSelector` is coupled to generation ratio catalog (`ASPECT_RATIO_TO_RESOLUTION`) and a specialized visualizer, indicating feature-specific semantics despite shared placement.
findings.suggestion: Relocate these components under feature/domain UI packages (for example `src/domains/generation/components/...`) and leave `shared/components` for truly cross-feature UI atoms/composites.

findings.summary: Hook tests target legacy module paths, signaling unresolved package-boundary migration drift.
evidence: Tests mock `@/shared/lib/errorHandler` and `@/shared/lib/queryKeys`, while implementations import `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError` and nested query-key modules.
This mismatch implies compatibility aliases or stale structure expectations, reducing clarity about canonical package boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one canonical path structure and update tests to mock the same modules the implementation imports; remove/deprecate legacy aliases after migration.

findings.summary: Error notification bootstrap is documented as required but runtime behavior hides missing registration.
evidence: README states notifier wiring occurs once in app bootstrap and shared modules should not assume toasts unless bootstrap has run.

`handleError` computes `shouldShowToast` and calls `emitErrorNotification`, but `emitErrorNotification` returns immediately when `window` is unavailable and has no guard/log when notifier was never configured.
`configureErrorNotifier` only throws when a different notifier is registered later; it does not enforce initial registration before first `handleError` call.
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit contract check path (e.g., dev-mode warning/error metric on first toast-eligible `handleError` when notifier is unconfigured) and codify bootstrap verification in a startup quality gate test.

findings.summary: Supabase client ownership policy is documented but not strongly encoded as an architectural boundary.
evidence: README defines `getSupabaseClient()` as canonical usage and says legacy proxy access should remain isolated.

`client.ts` provides a clean singleton API but no enforcement mechanism (lint boundary, restricted exports, or compatibility marker) to prevent direct construction/import of alternate clients outside this module.
The quality gate in this batch relies on docs for policy intent rather than an executable contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce enforceable boundary controls (e.g., eslint import restrictions to block direct Supabase bootstrap imports outside integration layer, plus a contract test asserting singleton access path) and reference those checks in README.

findings.summary: Modal callback contract is misrepresented: `onClose` is typed as no-arg but used as an `onOpenChange(boolean)` handler.
evidence: DescriptionModal renders `<Dialog open={isOpen} onOpenChange={onClose}>`.
DescriptionModalProps declares `onClose: () => void`, while Dialog `onOpenChange` receives an open-state boolean.
findings.suggestion: Change DescriptionModal to adapt the dialog callback explicitly, e.g. `onOpenChange={(open) => { if (!open) onClose(); }}` and keep `onClose` as no-arg, or retype prop as `(open: boolean) => void` if full open-state propagation is intended.

findings.summary: Adjacent-page preloading suppresses failures silently, breaking the project’s otherwise explicit error-reporting pattern.
evidence: Both prefetch branches in useAdjacentPagePreloader use empty `catch {}` blocks (one with comment-only ignore).
Other non-fatal async paths in assigned files route failures through `handleError(..., { showToast: false })` for consistent logging/context.
findings.suggestion: Replace silent catches with non-intrusive reporting via `handleError(error, { context: 'useAdjacentPagePreloader.prefetchNext|prefetchPrev', showToast: false })` to preserve observability without user-noise.

findings.summary: Multiple task builders duplicate the same orchestration/error-wrapping pipeline instead of sharing a reusable abstraction.
evidence: Each module repeats nearly identical lifecycle: validate inputs, resolve derived params, build payload, call `createTask`, catch `handleError`, then rethrow.
This duplication appears across separate task families with only payload details varying.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared `createTaskWithHandling`/pipeline helper that takes typed validators and payload builders, centralizing error context wrapping and reducing repeated orchestration scaffolding.

findings.summary: Inline edit state construction uses many pass-through layers that increase navigation cost without proportional simplification.
evidence: useInlineEditState is split into numerous helper hooks/functions that primarily forward arguments and remap fields.
Large adapter objects (e.g., image edit value models) are assembled by manually copying many properties, creating a broad indirection surface to trace behavior.
findings.suggestion: Collapse thin wrapper layers around strongly-related concerns (mode/canvas/form state), and expose fewer adapter objects with tighter typed sub-models so call chains are shorter and ownership is clearer.

findings.summary: A critical orchestration boundary is represented as `Record<string, unknown>`, hiding contract drift from TypeScript.
evidence: buildOrchestrationContract returns `Record<string, unknown>` rather than a specific contract type.
Downstream payload builders rely on stable keys like `task_family`, `run_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`, `based_on`, and `create_as_generation` across multiple task families.
findings.suggestion: Define and export an explicit `OrchestrationContract` interface (and narrow input/output types) so producers and consumers share compile-time guarantees for required/optional fields and naming.

findings.summary: Error emission channels differ by environment and module, causing uneven observability and context fidelity.
evidence: imageGeneration logs rejected batch items via DEV-only `console.error`, while other failures use `handleError`.
Supabase functions use direct `console.error` response paths rather than a shared structured error helper, unlike client-side conventions.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on structured error utilities per runtime (client/server) with consistent context fields; avoid branching between console-only and structured paths for equivalent failure classes.
